qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed frame followed by a normal second poll panics capnp-futures ReadStream
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about `capnp_futures::ReadStream::poll_next` retaining and re-polling a completed future after an error, while the advisory targets the safe APIs `constant::Reader::get` and `StructSchema::new` in `capnp/src/constant.rs` and `capnp/src/schema.rs` calling `PointerReader::get_root_unchecked` on unvalidated data.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause in the safe constructors `constant::Reader::get` or `StructSchema::new`; the session's only qualifying result is a separate async `ReadStream` repoll panic in `capnp-futures`.
evidence: The advisory summary and referenced fixes identify unsafe use of `PointerReader::get_root_unchecked` from the safe APIs `constant::Reader::get` and `StructSchema::new`, leading to undefined behavior from arbitrary words or encoded schema nodes.

The only qualifying finding, `read_stream_repoll_panic`, cites `capnp-futures/src/read_stream.rs:29`, `:73`, and `:76` and describes a completed future being re-polled after `try_read_message()` returns an error on malformed input.

That finding's reported source, sink, and exploit path do not involve `capnp/src/constant.rs`, `capnp/src/schema.rs`, `constant::Reader::get`, `StructSchema::new`, or unchecked schema/pointer construction, so it is not the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause.
